prd.oneLiner: Connect items, processes, work orders, output, quality, equipment, exception actions, and production reporting in an MES flow.
prd.goal: Reduce delays in production-output collection and improve traceability across process, quality, and delivery.
prd.whyNow: Production plans, work orders, process output, quality inspection, and equipment status are disconnected, so teams identify shop-floor exceptions and delivery risk too late.
prd.problem: Production plans, work orders, process output, quality inspection, and equipment status are disconnected, so teams identify shop-floor exceptions and delivery risk too late.
prd.solution: Connect items, processes, work orders, output, quality, equipment, exception actions, and production reporting in an MES flow.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: Shop-floor worker and production manager managing work orders and process output
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: Production plans, work orders, process output, quality inspection, and equipment status are disconnected, so teams identify shop-floor exceptions and delivery risk too late.

targets.name: Production operations and smart-factory coordinator
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: Production plans, work orders, process output, quality inspection, and equipment status are disconnected, so teams identify shop-floor exceptions and delivery risk too late.

targets.name: System and policy administrator
targets.needs: Manage access, policy, and audit history.
targets.pain: Production plans, work orders, process output, quality inspection, and equipment status are disconnected, so teams identify shop-floor exceptions and delivery risk too late.

scenarios.text: An operational user registers required work and checks its status.
An operator processes queued work and handles exceptions.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and access.
System and policy administrator reviews audit records and operating metrics.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Reduce delays in production-output collection and improve traceability across process, quality, and delivery.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of state changes against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of records connected to reporting

inScope: Item, bill of materials, process, work order, and quality-standard registration
Production plan, work progress, equipment, defect, work-in-progress, and shipment-history lookup
Work-order release, process execution, output collection, quality inspection, exception action, and completion handling
Production volume, defect rate, process lead time, equipment utilization, delivery, and traceability reporting
Item, bill of materials, process, work order, and quality-standard registration validation and duplicate prevention
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
Permission, policy, and state changes require audit logs.
